terms.definition: A scalar λ such that Av = λv for some non-zero vector v; the factor by which A scales that eigenvector direction.
A non-zero vector v whose direction is unchanged by multiplication by A, only scaled by the eigenvalue λ.
det(A - λI) as a polynomial in λ; its roots are the eigenvalues of A.
The set of all eigenvectors for a fixed eigenvalue λ, together with the zero vector; equals the null space of A - λI.
Writing A = PDP⁻¹ where D is diagonal with eigenvalues on the diagonal and columns of P are eigenvectors.
